results_a.name: version
results_a.stdout:
taccl 0.3.0 (Sections 1-9 - Device Discovery, Info, State Controls, Boot, Commands, Script Variables, Utility)
QTAC: 5.5.2
TAC:  7.0.1

results_a.name: help
results_a.stdout:
taccl - command-line interface for TAC devices
Usage: C:\ProdTools\qtac-cli\tools\taccl\build\Release-noqt\Release\taccl.exe [OPTIONS] SUBCOMMAND

Options:
  -h,--help                   Print this help message and exit
  --version                   Display program version information and exit
  -j,--json                   Output in JSON format
  -q,--quiet                  Suppress non-essential output

Subcommands:
  list                        List all connected TAC devices
  version                     Show QTAC and TAC library versions
  info                        Show device information
  set                         Configure device settings
  battery                     Battery power (query/set)
  usb0                        USB0 line (query/set)
  usb1                        USB1 line (query/set)
  power-key                   Power key (query/set)
  volume-up                   Volume-up key (query/set)
  volume-down                 Volume-down key (query/set)
  disconnect-uim1             Disconnect UIM1 (query/set)
  disconnect-uim2             Disconnect UIM2 (query/set)
  disconnect-sdcard           Disconnect SD card (query/set)
  disconnect-headset          Disconnect headset (query/set)
  primary-edl                 Primary EDL (query/set)
  secondary-edl               Secondary EDL (query/set)
  force-pshold                Force PS-Hold high (query/set)
  secondary-pm-resin          Secondary PM RESIN_N (query/set)
  eud                         EUD (query/set)
  external-power              External power (set-only)
  pin                         Raw pin state (set-only)
  boot                        Trigger a boot sequence on the device
  commands                    List available commands on the device
  command                     Query or set a dynamic command state
  quick-commands              List quick commands available on the device
  vars                        List all script variables on the device
  var                         Get or set a script variable
  help-text                   Retrieve device help text
  queue-clear                 Check if command queue is clear
  logging                     Get or set TACDev logging state

results_a.name: help_battery
results_a.stdout:
Battery power (query/set)
Usage: C:\ProdTools\qtac-cli\tools\taccl\build\Release-noqt\Release\taccl.exe battery [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -h,--help                   Print this help message and exit
  -d,--device TEXT            Device port name
  -s,--serial TEXT            Device serial number
  --state TEXT:{on,off}       on|off (omit to query current state)

results_a.name: help_usb0
results_a.stdout:
USB0 line (query/set)
Usage: C:\ProdTools\qtac-cli\tools\taccl\build\Release-noqt\Release\taccl.exe usb0 [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -h,--help                   Print this help message and exit
  -d,--device TEXT            Device port name
  -s,--serial TEXT            Device serial number
  --state TEXT:{on,off}       on|off (omit to query current state)

results_a.name: help_pin
results_a.stdout:
Raw pin state (set-only)
Usage: C:\ProdTools\qtac-cli\tools\taccl\build\Release-noqt\Release\taccl.exe pin [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -h,--help                   Print this help message and exit
  -d,--device TEXT            Device port name
  -s,--serial TEXT            Device serial number
  --state TEXT:{on,off} REQUIRED
                              on|off (omit to query current state)
  --pin INT REQUIRED          Pin number

results_a.name: ftdi_commands
results_a.stdout:
TC_READY_N;SW must program it to 1 to enable some output signals. False - Disables the control on some of the FTDI pins: DDBUS 0/2/4/7, CDBUS 0/2/4/7. True - Enable the control on all the FTDI pins.;30;<select a group>;;-1,-1
battery;Battery power off/on;25;General;Connections;0,0
eud;Embedded USB Debug (EUD);28;General;Switches;0,4
headset;Disconnects headset;18;General;Switches;0,5
pedl;Primary Emergency Download Mode;29;General;Switches;0,1
pkey;Power On (VOL_UP + PWR_ON = Held for boot to UEFI menu);27;General;Buttons;0,0
pshold;Force PS_HOLD to high;16;General;Switches;0,2
sdcard;Disconnects the SD Card;20;General;Switches;0,3
sedl;Secondary Emergency Download Mode;21;Fusion;Switches;0,0
sresn;Fusion Secondary PM_RESIN_N;23;Fusion;Switches;0,1
uim1;Disconnects the UIM 1;17;General;Switches;0,0
uim2;Disconnects the UIM 2;19;General;Switches;1,0
usb0;Disconnects VBUS0;31;General;Connections;1,0
usb1;Disconnects VBUS 1;22;General;Connections;2,0
voldn;(PM_RESIN_N) (Held to boot to fastboot);26;General;Buttons;0,2
volup;VOL_UP + PWR_ON = Held for boot to UEFI menu;24;General;Buttons;0,1
